items.title: Montrealers mark 150th anniversary of Mount Royal Park
items.summary_auto: It’s been a gathering place for generations of Montrealers, and it’s celebrating a milestone birthday. Mount Royal Park — a green landmark that’s synonymous with the city — is turning 150 years old.

items.title: 113-year-old Calgary building up for donation as city looks to preserve history of former Chinese laundry
items.summary_auto: Once destined for demolition, a historic building in southeast Calgary could soon find a new home. The City of Calgary says it is willing to donate the 113-year-old Ogden Block building, once home to a Chinese laundry and boarding house, to "a qualified party able to relocate the building" to make way for construction of the Green Line.

items.title: Caroline Mulroney resigning from Ford government's cabinet, Ontario legislature
items.summary_auto: Mulroney was elected in 2018 as part of Premier Doug Ford's first government and has served in a number of cabinet portfolios, including her current roles as Treasury Board president and francophone minister. In a letter Monday announcing her decision, she cited a desire to start a new chapter.

items.title: Pope Leo warns that AI challenges must be confronted with regulation, transparency in his 1st encyclical
items.summary_auto: Pope Leo urged governments to slow down the development of AI systems in the first enyclical of his papacy, warning that they spread misinformation, prioritize conflict and risk leading the world down a path of unending war.

items.title: Will the U.S. invade Cuba?
items.summary_auto: Following the indictment of former Cuban president Raúl Castro, is the U.S. preparing for a Venezuela-style invasion?

items.title: Iran and U.S. play down hopes for imminent breakthrough in war
items.summary_auto: Iran and the United States played down hopes for an imminent breakthrough in the three-month-old war on Monday, with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio saying there will either be a good agreement or Washington would deal with the country in "another way."

items.title: We visited Nova Scotia’s only abandoned theme park to find out what’s still standing
items.summary_auto: The Municipality of the County of Annapolis, which owns the former Upper Clements Park land, is hoping it will finally find a developer for the site with an upcoming request for development proposals. The land has sat empty since 2019, when the park closed after 30 years in operation.

items.title: This Calgary woman won a $13K settlement — but says her paralegal kept the money
Netanyahu's future uncertain as Israel starts to prepare for national election
items.summary_auto: While Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has been focussed on “total victory” for Israel since the Hamas-led attack on the country on Oct. 7, 2023, his own victory in a coming national election is far from certain.

items.title: Can banning scalpers fix the ticketing industry?
items.summary_auto: A growing chorus of artists, music industry managers and politicians is calling for new laws to ban pro scalpers from reselling sports and concert tickets online at huge markups.

items.title: 2 people face charges after newborn found dead in wooded area near Halifax
#TheMoment students raced concrete canoes
items.summary_auto: Students on Toronto Metropolitan University's concrete canoe team tell The National about the moment they made and raced a concrete canoe against other teams across Canada in Moncton, N.B.

items.title: 3 flotilla activists detained in Israel arrive back in Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Three activists whose Gaza-bound aid flotilla was intercepted in international waters by Israeli forces have returned home to Vancouver, with one of them saying they went through four days of continuous beatings and torture.

items.title: First Nation warns of fake letter ordering B.C. homeowners to vacate property
items.summary_auto: The Sḵwx̱wú7mesh Úxwumixw (Squamish Nation) is condemning a fake letter that’s circulating among Britannia Beach residents, which claims the nation was asserting Indigenous title over their property and ordering them to leave.

items.title: Erskine-Smith nomination appeal dismissed by Ontario Liberal Party panel
items.summary_auto: A complaint alleging voting irregularities during a tense Ontario Liberal nomination meeting in Scarborough has been dismissed by a party arbitration committee.

items.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak complicated by aid cuts, armed rebels and anger
items.summary_auto: Arson attacks on Ebola treatment centres in the Democratic Republic of Congo underscore the serious challenges authorities face — including a backlash in local communities — as they try to stem an outbreak of the infectious disease that has been declared a global health emergency. On Sunday, Congolese authorities said suspected cases have now passed 900 in the east of the country, mainly in Ituri province, where the ongoing outbreak is centred.

items.title: Mantario Trail expected to reopen in late June after 'remarkable' progress in restoration: Manitoba Parks
items.summary_auto: A hiking trail in Manitoba's Whiteshell Provincial Park that closed and has been undergoing repairs after it was scorched in last year's wildfires is now expected to re-open in June.

items.title: Kimi Antonelli claims Canadian Grand Prix for 4th straight F1 win
items.summary_auto: Formula One leader Kimi Antonelli charged to victory in a wild Canadian Grand Prix on Sunday, claiming his fourth-straight race win after teammate George Russell exited due to engine failure.

items.title: Interlake First Nations say Manitoba didn't consult them before blocking access to traditional hunting lands
items.summary_auto: A provincial gravel blockade along an access road east of Lake St. Martin tumbled earlier this week, as a group of First Nations in Manitoba's Interlake region gathered to remove it and get answers about why it was put there in the first place.

items.title: Gatineau, Que. group launches petition after NHL blocks organizers from hosting Habs watch party
items.summary_auto: A Gatineau group has launched a petition after the NHL wouldn’t let the Canadiens game be broadcast at the Slush Puppie Centre in Gatineau because the arena overlaps with the Ottawa Senators’ territory.

items.title: California officials say potential crack on overheated chemical tank could lower risk
items.summary_auto: Orange County Fire Authority Capt. Wayhowe Huang said officials will be continuing to evaluate the tank on Sunday after emergency crews spotted the potential crack overnight. As of Sunday morning, he said it does not appear that any of the highly volatile chemicals in the tank have leaked.

items.title: 'We got to the finish line': 9-year-old P.E.I. boy enjoys homecoming after heart transplant
items.summary_auto: Nine-year-old Maverick Bishop returned home on Saturday after spending 20 months in Toronto waiting for, then recovering from, a heart transplant.
